content.full_text:
Bernard C. Barmann
County Counsel

Stephen D. Schuett
Assistant County Counsel

CHIEF DEPUTIES
Tom Clow
Planning & Public Works

John M. Gallagher
Administration & Justice

Martin R. Lee
Health & Human Services

Robert D. Woods
Litigation

DEPUTIES
John R. Irby
Bruce Divelbiss
Patricia Randolph
Holly N. Gallagher
Mark L. Nations
Patrick L. Enright
Donna S. Weisman
Marvin R. Coston
Kirk B. Perkins
Margo A. Raison
Steven L. Schad
Charles F. Collins
Jeanne M. Desmarais
Stacy J. Inman
Louis Garcia
Susan M. Gill
Arthur H. Curran III

OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL

ADMINISTRATION AND COURTS BUILDING
1415 TRUXTUN AVENUE, 5th FLOOR
BAKERSFIELD, CALIFORNIA 93301

TELEPHONE (805) 861-2326
TELECOPIER (805) 322-8653

October 1, 1990

Blanca Breeze, Esq.
Counsel-Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice Made Pursuant
to Government Code section 83314 and Section 18329
of Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations

Dear Ms. Breeze:

My office represents the Board of Retirement of Kern County
Employees' Retirement Association (hereafter "the Association"),
which is comprised of nine members and one alternate member
(hereafter "Board members"). The names and mailing addresses of
those members are attached. The Board of Retirement is the
governing board for the Retirement Association and is charged with
the management of the retirement system (Government Code section
31520) and the investment of its assets (Government Code section
31595).

You may recall that we had a fairly lengthy telephone conversation
on Friday, September 14, 1990, in which we discussed the effect of
the amendment to Government Code section 87200 on the disclosure
category applicable to the Board members. For the past several
years, the Association has required the filing of a statement of
economic interests on Form 731 pursuant to its Conflict of Interest
Code and the Board members are designated as persons required to
disclose financial interests in the following disclosure
categories:

Blanca Breeze
October 1, 1990
Page 2

(a) Investments in, and income from, business entities which
are, or may foreseeably be, invested in by Kern County Employees'
Retirement Association.

(b) Investments in, and income from, persons or business
entities engaged in buying or selling securities for the account
of Kern County Employees' Retirement Association. Conflict of
Interest Code of the Kern County Employees' Retirement Association.

The Association's Conflict of Interest Code incorporates Government
Code sections defining "immediate family", "income", and
"investment".

You advised orally that the Board members should continue to file
their annual statement of economic interests using Form 730 until
such time as the phrase "other public officials who manage public
investments" is defined by Commission regulation. Pursuant to that
oral advice, the annual statement due to Board members
in April, 1991 (covering calendar year 1990) will be filed on a
Form 730 in accordance with the Association's Conflict of Interest
Code.

In the event that the Fair Political Practices Commission adopts
proposed regulation 18720 prior to April, 1991, you advised
that the Board members may then be obligated to file their annual
statement using Form 721 if the regulation adopted defines "other
public officials who manage public investments" in a manner which
encompasses the Board members.

Accordingly, and notwithstanding the printed Manual and Form 721
prepared and distributed by the Commission which suggests that
public pension officials must file a Form 721 for the 1990 annual
statement, we understand that your position is that the Board
members should continue to file under Form 730 unless and until the
Commission adopts a regulation clearly bringing those Board members
within the definition of "public officials who manage public
investments".

The Board members have authorized me to make this request for a
formal written opinion (see Resolution 90-7, attached). In
addition, I have been specifically authorized by Bob Harrington,
who was absent from the meeting at which the Resolution was
adopted, to request this opinion on his behalf also. Accordingly,
we will appreciate your written opinion on whether the Board
members may file their statements of economic interests on Form 730
pursuant to the Association's Conflict of Interest Code until such
time as the Commission adopts a regulation defining the Board
members as "other public officials who manage public investments".

Blanca Breeze
October 1, 1990
Page 3

Your assistance and courtesy has been and is most appreciated.

Very truly yours,

B.C. Barmann, County Counsel

By [signature]
Patricia J. Randolph, Deputy County Counsel

Enclosures

PJR:pwm/pjr.13
fppccconf.ltr
90.1930

embedding.qa_text: This is a formal letter from the Office of the County Counsel of Kern County, California, dated October 1, 1990. The letter is addressed to Blanca Breeze, Esq., Counsel-Legal Division, Fair Political Practices Commission, Sacramento, CA. It responds to a request for formal written advice made pursuant to Government Code section 83314 and Section 18329 of Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. The letter states that the writer's office represents the Board of Retirement of Kern County Employees' Retirement Association (hereafter "the Association"), which consists of nine members and one alternate member (hereafter "Board members"). The names and mailing addresses of those members are attached. The Board of Retirement is the governing board for the Retirement Association and is charged with the management of the retirement system (Government Code section 31520) and the investment of its assets (Government Code section 31595). The writer recalls a previous telephone conversation on Friday, September 14, 1990, in which they discussed the effect of the amendment to Government Code section 87200 on the disclosure category applicable to the Board members. For the past several years, the Association has required the filing of a statement of economic interests on Form 731 pursuant to its Conflict of Interest Code and the Board members are designated as persons required to disclose financial interests in the following disclosure categories: (a) Investments in, and income from, business entities which are, or may foreseeably be, invested in by Kern County Employees' Retirement Association. (b) Investments in, and income from, persons or business entities engaged in buying or selling securities for the account of Kern County Employees' Retirement Association. Conflict of Interest Code of the Kern County Employees' Retirement Association. The Association's Conflict of Interest Code incorporates Government Code sections defining "immediate family", "income", and "investment". You advised orally that the Board members should continue to file their annual statement of economic interests using Form 730 until such time as the phrase "other public officials who manage public investments" is defined by Commission regulation. Pursuant to that oral advice, the annual statement due to be filed by Board members in April, 1991 (covering calendar year 1990) will be filed on a Form 730 in accordance with the Association's Conflict of Interest Code. In the event that the Fair Political Practices Commission adopts proposed regulation 18720 prior to April, 1991, you have advised that the Board members may then be obligated to file their annual statement using Form 721 if the regulation adopted defines "other public officials who manage public investments" in a manner which encompasses the Board members.
